raw_text:
CloudVerse Systems

Senior DevOps Engineer

LOCATION

San Francisco, CA (Remote-first,
Open to relocate)

TYPE

Full-time

COMPENSATION

$165,000 - $210,000 USD
annually

TECH STACK

Kubernetes

Docker

Terraform

AWS (EC2, ECS, RDS,
Lambda)

GCP (GKE, Compute Engine,
Cloud SQL)

GitHub Actions

Jenkins

Python

Bash

Prometheus & Grafana

About CloudVerse Systems: CloudVerse Systems is a mid-sized
cloud infrastructure and DevOps consulting firm founded in 2018,
specializing in helping enterprises modernize their deployment
pipelines and adopt containerized architectures. We work with
Fortune 500 companies and high-growth startups to architect, deploy,
and maintain highly available systems. Our team of 120+ engineers
is distributed across North America and Europe, and we pride
ourselves on mentoring the next generation of infrastructure
engineers while solving complex real-world challenges.

CloudVerse Systems is seeking a Senior DevOps Engineer to join

our Infrastructure Engineering team. In this role, you'll lead the
design and implementation of scalable CI/CD pipelines, manage
Kubernetes clusters serving millions of requests daily, and mentor
junior engineers in infrastructure best practices. You'll have
significant influence over our infrastructure decisions, owning
complex multi-region deployments and driving architectural
improvements across our platform. This is an ideal opportunity for
an experienced infrastructure engineer ready to take on greater
technical leadership and architectural responsibility.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Design, implement, and maintain production Kubernetes clusters
across AWS and GCP, ensuring 99.95% uptime and optimal
resource utilization

Lead the development and optimization of CI/CD pipelines using
GitHub Actions and Jenkins, reducing deployment time by 40%+
through automation and best practices

Architect and codify infrastructure as code using Terraform,
managing complex multi-environment deployments and
infrastructure versioning

Mentor and review code from junior DevOps engineers,
establishing infrastructure standards and conducting knowledge-
sharing sessions

Collaborate with backend and platform teams to troubleshoot
production incidents, implement monitoring solutions using
Prometheus and ELK stack, and establish SLOs/SLAs

Develop and maintain Python and Bash automation scripts for
operational tasks, cloud resource management, and incident
response workflows

Lead capacity planning initiatives and cost optimization efforts
across cloud environments, identifying opportunities to reduce
cloud spend by 20%+ without compromising performance

Infrastructure that scales with yourambition Participate in on-call rotation for critical infrastructure incidents
and implement long-term solutions to prevent recurrence

REQUIREMENTS

5-8 years of professional experience in DevOps, Site Reliability
Engineering, or Cloud Infrastructure roles

Advanced proficiency with Kubernetes and Docker; demonstrated
experience managing multi-environment, production-scale
container orchestration

Expert-level knowledge of infrastructure as code, specifically
Terraform, with experience managing 50+ resources across
environments

Strong programming skills in Python and Bash; ability to write
maintainable, tested automation scripts

Hands-on experience with AWS (EC2, ECS, RDS, VPC) or GCP
(Compute Engine, GKE, Cloud SQL) at scale

Experience designing and implementing CI/CD pipelines using
GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or equivalent platforms

Deep understanding of networking, DNS, load balancing, and
security best practices in cloud environments

BONUS POINTS

Experience with Prometheus, Grafana, ELK stack, or equivalent
monitoring and logging solutions

Familiarity with multi-cloud or hybrid-cloud architectures

Experience with service mesh technologies such as Istio or
Linkerd

Track record of leading architectural decisions or technical
mentoring of junior engineers

Experience with infrastructure cost optimization and FinOps
practices

BENEFITS

Competitive salary and equity package aligned with senior-level
contributions

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with
90% employer coverage

Professional development budget of $5,000/year for conferences,
certifications, and training

Flexible work arrangement with 3 days in office per week (when
in SF) or fully remote within North America

Generous paid time off: 20 days vacation, 10 sick days, and 5
volunteer days annually

Parental leave: 16 weeks fully paid for primary caregiver, 8 weeks
for secondary caregiver

 APPLY

Apply through our careers page at cloudverse-systems.com/careers
with your resume, cover letter, and a link to your GitHub profile or
portfolio. Please highlight 2-3 projects that demonstrate your
infrastructure automation or Kubernetes expertise. We review
applications on a rolling basis and aim to respond within 2 weeks.

Familiarity with multi-cloud or hybrid-cloud architectures
Experience with service mesh technologies such as Istio or
